sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.14 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: (a) Do there exist a variety$$of groups and a group$G$such that the coproduct in$$of two copies of$G$is embeddable in$G$, but the coproduct of three such copies is not? See (G.\,M.\,Bergman, , 18 (2007), 349--403). Given an embedding$G *_ G G$, one might expect the induced map$G*_ (G *_ G) G *_ G G$to be an embedding. But this is not automatic, because in a general group variety$$, a map$G *_ A G ...
